# awscc_wafv2_rule_group (Resource)

Contains the Rules that identify the requests that you want to allow, block, or count. In a RuleGroup, you also specify a default action (ALLOW or BLOCK), and the action for each Rule that you add to a RuleGroup, for example, block requests from specified IP addresses or block requests from specified referrers. You also associate the RuleGroup with a CloudFront distribution to identify the requests that you want AWS WAF to filter. If you add more than one Rule to a RuleGroup, a request needs to match only one of the specifications to be allowed, blocked, or counted.

## Statement nesting depth

This resource is generated from a depth-limited schema: rule statements support up to
**3 levels of `statement` nesting**, which allows **2 chained logical statements**
(`and_statement`, `or_statement`, `not_statement`) above a match statement — for
example `and_statement` → `not_statement` → `byte_match_statement`. The AWS WAF API
itself imposes no nesting limit, and the hand-written `aws_wafv2_rule_group` resource in
terraform-provider-aws currently supports one additional logical level.

Consequences of the depth limit:

* Configuration nested deeper than the schema cannot be expressed; deeper attributes
  are not part of the schema.
* **Importing a rule group whose rules nest deeper than the schema silently omits the
  deeper statement levels from Terraform state.** The rule group itself is not modified
  by the import, and `terraform plan` will report no changes, but the state does not
  fully describe the remote resource.
* Updating a rule whose statements were truncated at import can produce incorrect
  patches against the remote rule group. Rule groups that use deeper nesting should not be
  managed with this resource until it supports their full depth.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `capacity` (Number)
- `scope` (String) Use CLOUDFRONT for CloudFront RuleGroup, use REGIONAL for Application Load Balancer and API Gateway.
- `visibility_config` (Attributes) Visibility Metric of the RuleGroup. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--visibility_config))

### Optional

- `available_labels` (Attributes List) Collection of Available Labels. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--available_labels))
- `consumed_labels` (Attributes List) Collection of Consumed Labels. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--consumed_labels))
- `custom_response_bodies` (Attributes Map) Custom response key and body map. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--custom_response_bodies))
- `description` (String) Description of the entity.
- `monetization_config` (Attributes) Configures monetization for the web ACL or rule group. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--monetization_config))
- `name` (String) Name of the RuleGroup.
- `rules` (Attributes List) Collection of Rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))
- `tags` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tags))

### Read-Only

- `arn` (String) ARN of the WAF entity.
- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `label_namespace` (String) Name of the Label.
- `rule_group_id` (String) Id of the RuleGroup

Required:

- `cloudwatch_metrics_enabled` (Boolean)
- `metric_name` (String)
- `sampled_requests_enabled` (Boolean)

- `currency_mode` (String) The currency mode for monetization. Use REAL for production payments and TEST for testing with testnet currencies.

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Optional:

- `action` (Attributes) Action taken when Rule matches its condition. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--action))
- `captcha_config` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--captcha_config))
- `challenge_config` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--challenge_config))
- `name` (String) Name of the RuleGroup.
- `priority` (Number) Priority of the Rule, Rules get evaluated from lower to higher priority.
- `rule_labels` (Attributes List) Collection of Rule Labels. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--rule_labels))
- `statement` (Attributes) First level statement that contains conditions, such as ByteMatch, SizeConstraint, etc (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement))
- `visibility_config` (Attributes) Visibility Metric of the RuleGroup. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--visibility_config))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--action"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.action`

Optional:

- `allow` (Attributes) Allow traffic towards application. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--action--allow))
- `block` (Attributes) Block traffic towards application. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--action--block))
- `captcha` (Attributes) Checks valid token exists with request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--action--captcha))
- `challenge` (Attributes) Checks that the request has a valid token with an unexpired challenge timestamp and, if not, returns a browser challenge to the client. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--action--challenge))
- `count` (Attributes) Count traffic towards application. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--action--count))
- `monetize` (Attributes) Monetize action for rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--action--monetize))

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.and_statement.statements.byte_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.and_statement.statements.rate_based_statement.custom_keys`

Optional:

- `asn` (String) Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `cookie` (Attributes) Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie))
- `forwarded_ip` (String) Specifies the first IP address in an HTTP header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `header` (Attributes) Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--header))
- `http_method` (String) Specifies the request's HTTP method as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ip` (String) Specifies the IP address in the web request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA4 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja4_fingerprint))
- `label_namespace` (Attributes) Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--label_namespace))
- `query_argument` (Attributes) Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_argument))
- `query_string` (Attributes) Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_string))
- `uri_path` (Attributes) Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--uri_path))

Optional:

- `field_to_match` (Attributes) Field of the request to match. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match))
- `regex_string` (String)
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.and_statement.statements.regex_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

(see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.and_statement.statements.size_constraint_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. - `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.and_statement.statements.xss_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.not_statement.statement.rate_based_statement.custom_keys`

Optional:

- `asn` (String) Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `cookie` (Attributes) Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie))
- `forwarded_ip` (String) Specifies the first IP address in an HTTP header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `header` (Attributes) Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--header))
- `http_method` (String) Specifies the request's HTTP method as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ip` (String) Specifies the IP address in the web request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA4 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja4_fingerprint))
- `label_namespace` (Attributes) Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--label_namespace))
- `query_argument` (Attributes) Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_argument))
- `query_string` (Attributes) Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_string))
- `uri_path` (Attributes) Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--uri_path))

- `match_scope` (String) The parts of the JSON to match against using the MatchPattern.
- `oversize_handling` (String) Handling of requests containing oversize fields

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.not_statement.statement.regex_pattern_set_reference_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.not_statement.statement.xss_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--not_statement--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.or_statement.statements.rate_based_statement.custom_keys`

Optional:

- `asn` (String) Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `cookie` (Attributes) Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie))
- `forwarded_ip` (String) Specifies the first IP address in an HTTP header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `header` (Attributes) Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--header))
- `http_method` (String) Specifies the request's HTTP method as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ip` (String) Specifies the IP address in the web request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA4 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja4_fingerprint))
- `label_namespace` (Attributes) Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--label_namespace))
- `query_argument` (Attributes) Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_argument))
- `query_string` (Attributes) Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_string))
- `uri_path` (Attributes) Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--uri_path))

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.or_statement.statements.regex_match_statement`

Optional:

- `field_to_match` (Attributes) Field of the request to match. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match))
- `regex_string` (String)
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.or_statement.statements.regex_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--body"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.or_statement.statements.regex_match_statement.field_to_match.body`

Optional:

- `oversize_handling` (String) Handling of requests containing oversize fields

- `oversize_handling` (String) Handling of requests containing oversize fields

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.or_statement.statements.size_constraint_statement`

Optional:

- `comparison_operator` (String)
- `field_to_match` (Attributes) Field of the request to match. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match))
- `size` (Number)
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.or_statement.statements.size_constraint_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--or_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

Optional:

- `custom_keys` (Attributes List) Specifies the aggregate keys to use in a rate-base rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys))
- `evaluation_window_sec` (Number)
- `forwarded_ip_config` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--forwarded_ip_config))
- `limit` (Number)

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.rate_based_statement.custom_keys`

Optional:

- `asn` (String) Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `cookie` (Attributes) Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie))
- `forwarded_ip` (String) Specifies the first IP address in an HTTP header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `header` (Attributes) Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--header))
- `http_method` (String) Specifies the request's HTTP method as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ip` (String) Specifies the IP address in the web request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA4 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja4_fingerprint))
- `label_namespace` (Attributes) Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--label_namespace))
- `query_argument` (Attributes) Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_argument))
- `query_string` (Attributes) Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_string))
- `uri_path` (Attributes) Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--uri_path))

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.rate_based_statement.custom_keys`

Optional:

- `asn` (String) Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `cookie` (Attributes) Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie))
- `forwarded_ip` (String) Specifies the first IP address in an HTTP header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `header` (Attributes) Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--header))
- `http_method` (String) Specifies the request's HTTP method as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ip` (String) Specifies the IP address in the web request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA4 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja4_fingerprint))
- `label_namespace` (Attributes) Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--label_namespace))
- `query_argument` (Attributes) Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_argument))
- `query_string` (Attributes) Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_string))
- `uri_path` (Attributes) Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--uri_path))

Optional:

- `field_to_match` (Attributes) Field of the request to match. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match))
- `regex_string` (String)
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.regex_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))

- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

Optional:

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.xss_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.and_statement.statements.xss_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--and_statement--statements--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.not_statement.statement.and_statement.statements.rate_based_statement.custom_keys`

Optional:

- `asn` (String) Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `cookie` (Attributes) Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie))
- `forwarded_ip` (String) Specifies the first IP address in an HTTP header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `header` (Attributes) Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--header))
- `http_method` (String) Specifies the request's HTTP method as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ip` (String) Specifies the IP address in the web request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA4 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja4_fingerprint))
- `label_namespace` (Attributes) Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--label_namespace))
- `query_argument` (Attributes) Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_argument))
- `query_string` (Attributes) Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_string))
- `uri_path` (Attributes) Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--not_statement--statement--and_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--uri_path))
