<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.or_statement.statements.rate_based_statement.custom_keys`

Optional:

- `asn` (String) Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `cookie` (Attributes) Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie))
- `forwarded_ip` (String) Specifies the first IP address in an HTTP header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `header` (Attributes) Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--header))
- `http_method` (String) Specifies the request's HTTP method as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ip` (String) Specifies the IP address in the web request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA4 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja4_fingerprint))
- `label_namespace` (Attributes) Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--label_namespace))
- `query_argument` (Attributes) Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_argument))
- `query_string` (Attributes) Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_string))
- `uri_path` (Attributes) Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--uri_path))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie"></a>

Optional:

- `regex_string` (String)
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.or_statement.statements.regex_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--body"></a>

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.or_statement.statements.size_constraint_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--or_statement--statements--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

- `custom_keys` (Attributes List) Specifies the aggregate keys to use in a rate-base rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys))
- `evaluation_window_sec` (Number)
- `forwarded_ip_config` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--forwarded_ip_config))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.rate_based_statement.custom_keys`

Optional:

- `asn` (String) Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `cookie` (Attributes) Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie))
- `forwarded_ip` (String) Specifies the first IP address in an HTTP header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `header` (Attributes) Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--header))
- `http_method` (String) Specifies the request's HTTP method as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ip` (String) Specifies the IP address in the web request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA4 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja4_fingerprint))
- `label_namespace` (Attributes) Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--label_namespace))
- `query_argument` (Attributes) Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_argument))
- `query_string` (Attributes) Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_string))
- `uri_path` (Attributes) Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--uri_path))

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.byte_match_statement`

Optional:

- `field_to_match` (Attributes) Field of the request to match. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match))
- `positional_constraint` (String) Position of the evaluation in the FieldToMatch of request.
- `search_string` (String) String that is searched to find a match.
- `search_string_base_64` (String) Base64 encoded string that is searched to find a match.
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.byte_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--byte_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

Optional:

- `asn` (String) Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `cookie` (Attributes) Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--cookie))
- `forwarded_ip` (String) Specifies the first IP address in an HTTP header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `header` (Attributes) Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--header))
- `http_method` (String) Specifies the request's HTTP method as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ip` (String) Specifies the IP address in the web request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--ja3_fingerprint))

- `label_namespace` (Attributes) Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. - `query_argument` (Attributes) Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. - `query_string` (Attributes) Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--query_string))
- `uri_path` (Attributes) Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--custom_keys--uri_path))

Optional:

- `regex_string` (String)
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.regex_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.regex_pattern_set_reference_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.sqli_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.regex_pattern_set_reference_statement`

- `arn` (String) ARN of the WAF entity.
- `field_to_match` (Attributes) Field of the request to match. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match))
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.regex_pattern_set_reference_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.sqli_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--sqli_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.regex_pattern_set_reference_statement`

Optional:

- `arn` (String) ARN of the WAF entity.
- `field_to_match` (Attributes) Field of the request to match. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match))
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.regex_pattern_set_reference_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_wafv2_rule_group.example
  identity = {
    name  = "name"
    id    = "id"
    scope = "scope"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `id` (String) Id of the RuleGroup
- `name` (String) Name of the RuleGroup
- `scope` (String) Use CLOUDFRONT for CloudFront RuleGroup, use REGIONAL for Application Load Balancer and API Gateway

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_wafv2_rule_group.example
  id = "name|id|scope"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_wafv2_rule_group.example "name|id|scope"
```
